name: Write Config Pages

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Write Config Pages is complete and correct

scope: Author the four configuration documentation pages: config-quickstart.md (five-minute getting-started), configuration.md (rewritten how-it-works guide covering tiers, merge semantics, common customizations, CLI commands), config-reference.md (every config field by section with type/default/description/example), and task-classes.md (dedicated class system page with language/framework tables, custom classes, fallback behavior). Each page is a separate leaf. Cross-references between pages should be wired as each page is written.
success_criteria: All four documentation files exist: config-reference.md, task-classes.md, configuration.md (rewritten), config-quickstart.md
Every config field in the Config struct appears in config-reference.md with type, default, description, and example
Every built-in class from Defaults() appears in task-classes.md with its key and description
Cross-references between pages are wired: quickstart links to guide and task-classes, guide links to reference and task-classes, reference links to guide, task-classes links to guide and reference
No invented fields or classes — every documented item traces back to the Config struct or Defaults()

breadcrumbs.text: Created 4 leaf children: config-reference, task-classes, configuration-guide, config-quickstart. Ordering: reference first (field-level foundation other pages link into), task-classes second (self-contained topic referenced by guide and quickstart), configuration-guide third (rewritten how-it-works page referencing both), config-quickstart last (links to all three). Spec .wolfcastle/docs/specs/2026-03-22T06-00Z-config-docs-overhaul.md and task-classes spec 2026-03-15T00-04Z consulted. Sibling deep-linking-pass runs after all pages exist.

escalations.description: gofmt violation in internal/daemon/coverage_test.go predates this node
